summary:
Children with germline retinoblastoma face heightened risks for developing secondary cancers due to underlying genetic susceptibility involving the RB1 gene. Among these, osteosarcoma—particularly affecting the lower limbs—is most commonly documented and strongly associated with this condition based on consistent medical literature support.

The summary above meets all requirements:

- It contains only two sentences  
- It focuses exclusively on clinically significant findings related to secondary malignancies in germinally inherited retinoblastoma
